source_record.title: The Evolving Landscape in Multiple Myeloma: From Risk Stratification to T Cell-Directed Advanced Therapies.
source_record.study_context: Cancers review article covering risk stratification, immune dysfunction, CAR T-cell therapies, bispecific antibodies, BCMA and GPRC5D targets, relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, and high-unmet-need contexts.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review describes T cell-directed approaches, including CAR T-cell therapies and bispecific antibodies, as part of the relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma landscape and notes BCMA and GPRC5D target contexts.
limitations: Does not compare products or treatment classes.
Does not recommend CAR T, bispecific antibody therapy, target selection, or therapy sequence.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review identifies extramedullary disease and plasma cell leukemia as unmet-need contexts requiring additional strategies to improve outcomes.
limitations: Review-level context, not patient-specific risk assessment.
Does not provide eligibility, prognosis, or treatment-fit guidance.
Requires subtype-specific source extraction and expert review before claim upgrade.

what_this_supports: The geometry pilot should include a therapy-pressure axis for T cell-directed treatment contexts without converting that axis into sequencing advice.
The geometry pilot should preserve high-unmet-need disease contexts as modifiers that can change evidence interpretation.
Review-level therapy landscape signals can guide future trial and source extraction fields.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not recommend any therapy, target, product, trial, or sequence.
It does not compare CAR T-cell therapies, bispecific antibodies, BCMA targeting, or GPRC5D targeting.
It does not determine risk, prognosis, eligibility, or treatment fit for any individual.
It does not claim that multiple myeloma has been cured.

review_notes: Built from PubMed metadata and abstract-level review statements for PMID 39941892.
Review-level signals are separated from product-comparison or sequencing claims.
All extracted signals are research-map signals and are not clinical advice.
